steps.title: Identify the grammatical role the blank must fill
steps.content: The blank sits inside a relative clause introduced by which. Because which is the subject of that clause, the blank must be the finite verb — the main predicate that makes the clause a complete grammatical unit.

steps.title: Eliminate each non-finite form, then confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — provided: A finite past-tense verb; correctly serves as the predicate of the relative clause. ✓
  • Choice B — having provided: A non-finite perfect participle; cannot be the main verb of a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — to provide: A non-finite infinitive; cannot be the main verb of a clause. ✗
  • Choice D — providing: A non-finite present participle; cannot be the main verb of a clause. ✗
A is correct.
